title: Recrutement: Building the 1209-User Core
sources: README.md: Role definitions and recruitment philosophy [cite: 2]
database.sql: User population limits and database schema
quests.ts: Foundational quest specifications for District B
App.tsx: Quest reward and economic logic implementation
memories.ts: Memory data structure and archival rules
resume: The quest "Recrutement" (B2) is a fundamental operation within the Équipe district, mapping to the Quête action at coordinate B2 on the 10x10 FRM grid. As a pillar of Phase 1: The Foundations (V1), this quest establishes the human pipeline required to reach the planned limit of 1209 total users. This population is strategically tiered into 9 Super-Moderators, 50 Moderators, 150 Creators, and 1000 Associates to ensure a balanced ecosystem. Unlike traditional social networks where accounts are created instantly, the Forom philosophy mandates that users, especially those seeking the "Creator" title, must earn their status by completing a Quest and having their memo selected for the official archive[cite: 2]. This meritocratic approach ensures that every member of the team is aligned with the mission of saving long-term community memory[cite: 2]. Technically, new users are managed via the "users" table in the local PostgreSQL database, which tracks their username, role, and currency. The system uses invite keys and claimed status to maintain a private, invite-only "Private Era" during the node's early development[cite: 2]. Each successful recruitment action is recorded as a memo, reinforcing the community's structural data for future RAG ingestion. Economically, the recruitment quest is incentivized by the Pixel (PX) system; in V1, completing this quest rewards the contributor with 2 PX[cite: 2]. This reward facilitates the redistribution of purchasing power, which begins with the 500 PX held by each Super-Mod[cite: 2]. The software stack, powered by React 19 and Vite 7, provides a clear interface for tracking these quests and role transitions within the spatial grid[cite: 2]. By hosting this recruitment data on local hardware like an NVIDIA Jetson, the community maintains absolute sovereignty over its social graph[cite: 2]. This is critical for pilot projects like the ETS Clubs, where knowledge transfer between graduating generations depends on a clear, documented history of who held which role[cite: 2]. The B2 quest ensures that the "Deep Lore" includes the origins and contributions of every team member, creating an immutable human record[cite: 2]. This record eventually feeds into ROM in Phase 3, allowing the AI to understand the community's human architecture[cite: 2]. Ultimately, B2 is the gateway for sustainable growth within the matrix. It guarantees that the population check required for the Phase 3 ritual is met accurately.
summary: This quest facilitates the meritocratic recruitment of community members at coordinate B2, ensuring the 1209-user population limit is reached through verified contributions.
